messages.content: You are an expert EU proposal writer for IRIS Technology Solutions, a photonics and NIR spectroscopy company. Write in first-person plural (we/our). Use precise technical language. Follow EU Horizon proposal conventions: numbered tasks (**Task X.Y**), clear objectives, evidence-based claims. Provide a comprehensive analysis of the current state of the art, identify gaps, and explain how this project advances beyond existing knowledge.
Write the State of the art and innovation section for a Horizon Europe proposal.

1.2 RELATION TO WORK PROGRAMME  
The table below provides an overview of the HORIZON -CL4-2021-DIGITAL-EMERGING-01-09: AI, data and Robotics for the Green Deal (IA) scope and how we address it in our proposed work.  
Table 1.2 Relation of RECLAIM to HORIZON-CL4-2021-DIGITAL-EMERGING-01-09: AI, data and Robotics for the Green Deal (IA).  
Call text “... integrate and optimise AI, data and robotics solutions ... to demonstrate ... in actual or highly realistic operating environments, how they directly contribute to the Green Deal.[...]”  
We generate new AI-driven computer vision models trained with real data, and a distributed multi-robot multi-gripper system for material recovery. The developed technology will be demonstrated in two real waste treatment use-cases to undertake material recovery and thus explicitly contribute to the Green Deal.  
Objective 3, 4 – WP 3, 4, 5  
Call text “... supported by industry or service relevant KPIs,.. and demonstrating scalability, and deployment potential.  
We are supported by measurable service relevant KPIs (see Objectives). The development of multiple use cases with distinct characteristics demonstrates the scalability and proves the deployment potential of the developed technology. Objective 3, 4 - WP 5  
Call text “The environmental impacts of the proposed solutions should also be taken into account…”  
We develop an eco-friendly prMRF that can be dynamically (re)configured based on environmental and economic criteria. The environmental impact of prMRF will be explicitly assessed and maximized.  
Objective 1 - WP 2, 5  
Call text “... focus... on real-world scenarios, which can benefit in short to mid-term from the technology and solutions and demonstrate substantial impact on the Green Deal..”  
We introduce a new effective solution for material recovery in sectors with currently inefficient waste treatment thus accomplishing substantial positive impact on the Green Deal. We demonstrate the application of the developed technology in real world, difficult and highly demanding material recovery scenarios.  
Objective 1, 5 - WP 2, 5  
Call text “while taking into account the maturity of the technologies to solve the problems at hand”  
We exploit available, well tested technologies that are further improved and integrated into a new high-tech solution for decentralized, close to source material recovery. Objective 3 - WP 3, 6  
Call text “Deep involvement of all relevant stakeholders..”  
Our consortium involves a wide range of different stakeholders (Universities, RTOs, SMEs, NGOs, Local Authorities) who are particularly interested in the results of our project and are fully committed to its success. Objective 6 - WP 1, 7  
Call text “Special attention will be given to including users of diverse age, gender and background.”  
The implementation of reality-inspired environmental games and particularly the RDG provides an effective mechanism for involving users of diverse age, gender and background in the research activities of our project.  
Objective 5 - WP 1, 6  
Call text “incorporate training programs for non-expert users of AI, data and robotics systems... including the basics concepts of Trustworthy and ethical AI.”  
We incorporate training sessions for the staff of the end-user participants to inform users who are not experts in AI, robotics and data management about the possibilities offered by new technologies, which are secured and framed by trustworthy and ethical AI. Objective 1 - WP 1  
                                                     
26 A. Liapis: "Mixed-initiative Creative Drawing with webIconoscope," In Proceedings of the 6th International Conference on Computational Intelligence in Music, Sound, Art and Design (EvoMusArt), vol. 10198, LNCS. Springer, 2017.
